proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1 – Precondition Propagation (printHelp has no pre‑conditions)
; ---------------------------------------------------------------
; Model the call to `printHelp` with an integer flag `called` (1 = invoked, 0 = not invoked).
; The log statement on line 528 is unconditional, therefore the execution flag
; `line528_executed` must become 1 whenever `called` is 1.
; To *prove* the statement always runs we assert the negation:
;   called = 1 ∧ line528_executed = 0
; This combination is impossible, so Z3 should return `unsat`.
(declare-const called Int)           ; 1 ⇔ printHelp was invoked
(declare-const line528_executed Int) ; 1 ⇔ the console.log on line 528 ran

; printHelp is invoked → the line is executed (unconditional)
(assert (= line528_executed (ite (= called 1) 1 0)))

; Negate the property we want to prove:
;   printHelp is called (called = 1) but the line does NOT run (line528_executed = 0)
(assert (= called 1))
(assert (= line528_executed 0))

(check-sat)   ; expected: unsat  → the line is guaranteed to execute when printHelp is called
